# Apple HIG compliance (iOS)

> **Project-scoped.** Loaded on-demand by **planning** (so the plan chooses native patterns)
> and at the **design seam** (`claude-design-to-swiftui`, DS-plan design-review) — the map
> that turns a web design's idioms into iOS-native equivalents rather than a web layout in a
> native shell. Complements `swift-conventions` (the *how*); this is the *what/why* of
> platform fit. HIG is a guideline, not a linter — most items below are reviewed by a human,
> a few are App Store rejection risks (flagged ⚠, cross-ref `app-store-submission-requirements`).

## Why this exists at the design seam

Claude Design produces a **web** rendition. Faithful pixels are necessary but not
sufficient — an app that ignores platform conventions feels wrong and risks rejection.
This skill is the checklist for the "needs native mapping" section of `design-spec.md`:
for each web idiom, the native pattern to use instead.

## Navigation & structure

- **Tab bars** (`TabView`) for 2–5 top-level, peer sections — the iOS equivalent of a web
  top nav / sidebar. Don't hide primary navigation behind a hamburger menu (an Android/web
  idiom); surface it.
- **Navigation stack** with a title and a back affordance for hierarchical drill-in; large
  titles (`.navigationBarTitleDisplayMode(.large)`) for top-level screens, inline for depth.
- **Modality** — use a **sheet** for a self-contained subtask the user completes and
  dismisses; `.fullScreenCover` only when the task truly owns the screen. Web "modal for
  everything" maps to sheets sparingly.
- **Back/dismiss** — never build a custom back button that breaks the swipe-to-go-back
  gesture; preserve the interactive pop gesture.

## Layout, safe areas, adaptivity

- Respect **safe areas** (notch, Dynamic Island, home indicator); let backgrounds extend
  edge-to-edge with intent (`.ignoresSafeArea()`) but keep controls inside. Use
  `.safeAreaInset` for pinned bars.
- **Adapt across size classes** — iPhone portrait/landscape and iPad. A fixed web width must
  become flexible; on iPad prefer `NavigationSplitView` over a stretched phone layout when
  the design implies a sidebar.
- **Layout margins & readable width** — keep content off the edges (system margins) and
  constrain long text to a readable measure rather than full-bleed on iPad.

## Touch targets & interaction

- **≥ 44×44 pt** hit target for every interactive control (HIG minimum) — even if the web
  design draws a smaller button, expand the tappable area (`.frame(minWidth:44,minHeight:44)`
  or `.contentShape`). ⚠ tiny targets are a common usability rejection.
- **No hover-dependent affordances** — anything gated behind `:hover` on web must be visible
  or reachable by tap on iOS (see `claude-design-to-swiftui` un-mappable list).
- **Standard gestures** — swipe-to-delete in lists, pull-to-refresh (`.refreshable`),
  swipe-back; don't override system gestures with custom ones.
- **Haptics** for meaningful feedback (`.sensoryFeedback` iOS 17+), sparingly.

## Typography & color

- **Dynamic Type** — use text styles (`.body`, `.title`, …) or text-style-relative custom
  fonts so text scales with the user's setting; verify at the largest accessibility sizes.
  ⚠ text that can't scale is an accessibility failure.
- **SF Symbols** — prefer them for iconography (they scale, weight-match text, and adapt to
  appearance). Match symbol weight to adjacent text; use multicolor/hierarchical rendering
  where the design implies it.
- **Semantic colors** — use system semantic colors (`.primary`, `.secondary`,
  `Color(.systemBackground)`) or asset-catalog colors that answer light/dark and increased
  contrast; never freeze the web design's single light palette.
- **Dark mode is expected** — every screen must render correctly in both appearances.

## Motion & feedback

- Honor **Reduce Motion** — swap large transitions for fades when
  `accessibilityReduceMotion` is set. Map web `@keyframes`/parallax conservatively.
- Show **progress** for anything over ~1s (`ProgressView`); reflect loading/empty/error
  states explicitly — don't leave a blank screen (web spinners → SwiftUI `ProgressView` +
  proper empty/error views).

## Content & system integration (where relevant)

- **Localization & RTL** — String Catalogs; test right-to-left mirroring if shipping RTL.
- **System features** used only with a clear user benefit and the required usage-description
  string (⚠ missing `NSUsageDescription` = crash/rejection — see submission skill): camera,
  photos (`PHPicker`), location, notifications, Face ID.
- **Sign in with Apple** ⚠ — if the app offers third-party/social login, App Store rules
  generally require offering Sign in with Apple too. Flag in planning if any social login
  exists.

## The web→native mapping checklist (fill per design)

For each screen in `design-spec.md`, confirm:

- [ ] Top-level nav expressed as a **tab bar / native nav**, not a hamburger/web header.
- [ ] Every tap target ≥ 44×44 pt.
- [ ] No affordance depends on hover; pressed/selected states defined.
- [ ] Text uses Dynamic-Type-scaling fonts; checked at XXXL.
- [ ] Colors are semantic/asset-catalog; dark mode verified.
- [ ] Icons are SF Symbols where one fits.
- [ ] Modals mapped to sheets/covers appropriately; swipe-back preserved.
- [ ] Loading / empty / error states defined for every data-backed view.
- [ ] Safe areas respected; layout adapts to size classes (and iPad if targeted).
- [ ] Any system capability has its usage-description string planned.
- [ ] Motion honors Reduce Motion.

Unchecked items are **Open Questions** for the plan, not silent omissions.
